title: Maharashtra School Reopen: Schools Likely to Start on June 26 Instead of June 15 Due to Heatwave - PUNE PULSE
full_text:
May 26: The Maharashtra government is considerably postponing the reopening of schools in the Vidarbha region from June 15 to June 26 due to intense heatwave conditions and rising temperatures across several districts.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued red and orange heatwave alerts for several districts, including Akola, Amravati, Wardha, Nagpur, Yavatmal, and Chandrapur. Severe heat conditions have also been reported from Gadchiroli, Gondia, and Bhandara districts.
The rising concerns over students’ safety after the temperatures in parts of Vidarbha crossed levels. Brahmapuri in Chandrapur district recently recorded a scorching 47.2°C, making it one of the hottest places in the country.
Minister of State for School Education Pankaj Bhoyar reportedly stated that the government is closely monitoring the weather situation and that student safety remains the top priority. A final decision regarding the reopening dates is expected after June 10, following a review meeting by the School Education Department.
The ongoing heatwave has already affected educational activities in the region. Reports suggest that Nagpur University has shifted examinations to morning sessions to protect students from extreme afternoon temperatures. Authorities are also increasing the number of examination centres to make commuting easier for students during the heatwave.
Earlier, the Nagpur bench of the Bombay High Court had reportedly questioned the state government’s decision to reopen schools in Vidarbha from June 15 despite temperatures ranging between 42°C and 44°C. Parents’ associations and teachers’ groups have also demanded a delayed reopening, citing health and safety concerns for children.
If approved, the revised reopening date could provide relief to students, parents, and school staff dealing with the ongoing extreme weather conditions in the region.
